text: 3-Hydroxy-1H-phenalen-1-one (HPLN), chemical formula C13H8O2, molecular weight 196.20, is a single-component organic molecular crystal and a prototropic ferroelectric. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar monoclinic space group Pn (No. 7), with unit cell a = 9.088 Å, b = 17.707 Å, c = 11.604 Å, β = 93.765°, Z = 8. The α-polymorph exhibits ferroelectricity originating from cooperative proton transfer (prototropy), where a hydrogen atom is relocated and simultaneously the location of a single bond and that of an adjacent double bond are interchanged. Molecules are connected by intermolecular hydrogen bonding interactions; the non-centrosymmetric polar structure is compatible with piezoelectric properties. The crystal structure (CCDC 796166, CSD refcode TAPZIT01) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 293 K with an R factor of 4.15% and a calculated density of 1.399 g/cm³. Experimental studies have demonstrated both direct and converse piezoelectric effects using the Berlincourt method and laser interferometry. The experimentally measured longitudinal piezoelectric coefficient is d33 = 7.3 ± 0.8 pC/N at room temperature. The piezoelectric properties show positive correlation with spontaneous polarization in the prototropic ferroelectric series.
experimental_method: Direct piezoelectric measurement (Berlincourt method) and converse effect (laser interferometry)
property_ref_title: Piezoelectricity of strongly polarized ferroelectrics in prototropic organic crystals

comparison_with_other_materials.vs_ferroelectric_polymers: about half that of PVDF (12-30 pC/N)
comparison_with_other_materials.vs_piezoceramics: one or two orders of magnitude smaller than PZT (100-800 pC/N)
comparison_with_other_materials.vs_ferroelectric_salts: different behavior than Rochelle salt or TGS due to thermal stability

additional_notes: Non-centrosymmetric polar monoclinic space group (Pn) is crystallographically compatible with piezoelectricity
Ferroelectricity originates from cooperative proton transfer (prototropy/proton tautomerism)
Experimental crystal structure obtained from the Cambridge Structural Database (CCDC 796166)
Deposited structure corresponds to the ferroelectric α-polymorph used in piezoelectric investigations
Piezoelectricity experimentally confirmed using both direct (Berlincourt method) and converse (laser interferometry) measurements
Crystal consists of discrete neutral HPLN molecules held together by hydrogen bonding
Positive correlation observed between spontaneous polarization and piezoelectric coefficient in prototropic ferroelectric series
Modest piezoelectricity despite strong polarization, attributed to thermally robust ferroelectricity and ferroelectric switching mechanism that minimizes molecular deformations
Electromechanical coupling factor (k32) determined from piezoelectric resonance-antiresonance measurements
Suitable for inclusion in experimentally verified ferroelectric and piezoelectric molecular crystal database
